candidate: Likely a myxoid cyst.  See a dermatologist for confirmation and treatment options.
query_text: See picture.  Is this Mucoid cyst? It happens on the thumb, for 8 years already.  It is not itchy or painful.  It doesn't affect the sense there.  The size is 1 cm X 1 cm.  The nail has been deformed to a wavy shape becasue of the pressing.  Is this myxoid cyst?
gold_texts: ["The picture is very clear.  It is myxoid cyst.", "No.  This is cutaneous cysts at finger.  Need surgery + pathological testing.", "Impression: myxoid cyst. On the side, near the end of the thumb, there is a semi-spherical cyst 1 cm X 1 cm.  Color is normal, surface is smooth, shiny, soft and has the feeling of being a cyst. (After it is poked open, you should see sticky but  transparent liquid inside.  This may happen repeatedly). This disease mostly is caused by bruising or under pressure for an extended period of time.  Treatment: fill in small amount of triamcinolone acetonide after draining the sticky liquid inside.  But it may relapse.  The best way to handle it is to remove it surgically."]

query_text: Would experts here please help take a look. Would experts here take a look. Patient is 34 years old, female.  There grows figure like pattern all over the body, with the exception of palms and soles.  Has been like that for 20 years, and growing more.  Would experts here take a look and advise what disease it is, and the treatment.  Thanks.
gold_texts: ["Idiopathic punctate hypopigmentation", "Generalized punctate hypopigmentation", "Idiopathic punctate hypopigmentation.  No treatment needed.", "Generalized punctate hypopigmentation.  No treatment needed.", "The basic physiological change as a result of Idiopathic punctate hypopigmentation: a. The atrophy of the epidermis, the reduction in numbers of Melanin granules b. Basal melanocytes is decreasing.  DOPA response weakening. c.  With microscopy, it can be seen the melanosome is in stage I and II.  But the Dendritic process of melanocytes is normal.  Clinical Impression: a.  Onset of the disease mostly after middle age. b. White speck in round or irregular shape with clear boundary.  Diameter 2 - 6 mm.  Can be as many as 50 pieces.  The white specks are not fusing together, but can form a net like dense structure. c. There is no self detected symptom.  Diagnosis based on clinical data: Vitiligo  Nodular salinization  Guttate morphea  Hypopigmentation after having Sclerotic atrophic lichen inflammation Alias  Disseminate lenticular leucoderma, Disseminate reticulate leukoderma", "Generalized punctate hypopigmentation.", "Generalized punctate hypopigmentation", "Generalized punctate hypopigmentation"]

candidate: 这个洞型伤口需要及时清洁并消毒，使用无菌敷料覆盖，防止感染，并定期更换。如果伤口不愈合或有感染迹象，请尽快就医。
